observations.recommendation:
1.5. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the Conceptual Framework for General Purpose Financial Reporting by Public Sector Entities. PPSAS as to the recording of transactions –

a.) record transactions in the period they are incurred regardless whether they are paid or not;

b.) reflect transactions in the financial statements for contracts that have been completed prior to the end of the period; and,

c.) use proper account titles in the recording of transactions

1.6. We also recommended that the proposed adjusting entries in the Summary of Entries in Annex E be recorded.

2.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:

a.) stop recording 20% Development Fund through the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts; and

b.) identify the discrepancy between the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds totaling P8,344.67 and cause the necessary adjustments in the books in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements at year-end and eliminate the accounts during consolidation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.) request the Sanggunian to pass a Resolution or any similar issuances providing a detailed sharing scheme as authorized by PHIC Philhealth Circular No. 2024-002 for the performance incentives; and

b.) monitor and evaluate Capitation Fund utilization regularly to promote effective, needs-based resource allocation.

5.9. We recommended that the Municipality observe the time duration for the implementation and completion of projects downloaded from external agencies to avoid the return of funds due to breach of agreement.
6.4. We recommended that provisions in the MOA be observed by the Municipality with respect to external funds received to avoid breach, loss of confidence and disallowances.

7.7. We recommended that management review the corresponding Memorandum of Agreement of these projects to determine whether to return the fund to the source agencies or to request for the transfer of the unexpended balance to the general fund.
8.3. We recommended and Management agreed to submit the MOAs to the Audit Team after perfection thereof.

9.10. We recommended the following:

a.) The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to cease charging ineligible transactions to the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF). Only Programs, Projects, and Activities (PPAs) specifically enumerated under Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 should be included in the Local DRRM Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and the corresponding budget. Administrative and other operating expenses not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management should instead be sourced from the General Fund of the Local Government Unit (LGU);

b.) The Municipal Accountant to record the deficient balance under the Trust Liability – DRRMF account to properly reflect the accurate fund balance of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in the books of accounts; and

c.) Revert the retention of Rehabilitation of Malucsad to Amdalao Slope Protection amounting to ₱473,453.13, which was erroneously charged to the LDRRMF.

10.9. We recommended and Management agreed to:

a) Ensure that the National Food Authority (NFA) is considered as the primary source for rice procurement, in accordance with Section 1 of Executive Order No. 51. Future procurement activities involving rice should prioritize NFA as a supplier, unless a valid exemption is obtained and properly documented; and

b) In cases where procurement from sources other than NFA is deemed necessary, the BAC should conduct and document a thorough cost-benefit analysis and market survey to justify the procurement decision. This should include price comparisons and quality assessments to demonstrate that the chosen supplier offers the most advantageous terms for the government.

11.9. We recommended that Management strictly observe CSC MC No. 26 and stop the hiring of consultant or supervising JOs.

11.10. We likewise reiterate that Management implement COA-DBM-Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020 particularly emphasis on the following:

a.) Ensuring the necessity and reasonableness of hiring Job Order (JO) personnel, and strictly adhering to the established criteria for their selection; and

b.) Requiring the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to ensure that the duties and responsibilities assigned to JOs do not duplicate or overlap with those of permanent plantilla employees.

12.5. We recommended the following:

a.) demand immediate liquidation of the overdue cash advances by sending Demand Letters to concerned Accountable Officers;

b.) enjoin all officials and employees to strictly comply with the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period and ensure that all cash advances granted during the year be liquidated also on the same year, otherwise, their salaries should be withheld if they still fail to liquidate as at January 20 of the ensuing year; and

c.) Reclassify the improper recording of cash advances for foreign travel from Prepaid Expenses to Advances to Officers and Employees.
13.5. We recommended that management direct LGU officers and employees to strictly comply with the various provision of Executive Order No. 77 when undertaking travel.

14.6. We recommended that the LSB include the unutilized fund in succeeding budget preparation and to henceforth ensure that an annual budget is prepared in accordance with JC No.1, s. 2017 and to ensure strict compliance with the budget preparation timeline and reporting requirements as outlined. Immediate measures should be taken to assess and utilize the SEF in line with its mandated purpose, ensuring that essential educational PPAs are adequately funded and implemented.

15.4. We recommended and Management, thru the Accounting Office agreed to promptly establish and maintain subsidiary ledgers/registries to record all GAD-related expenses and financial transactions, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2021-008, in order to facilitate the monitoring, accounting and preparation of the GAD-related reports.
16.7. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or Accountable Officer to check and carefully review the posted amount to any official documents before it is issued to payee.

17.6. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer constantly monitor the management and issuance of Official Receipts in strict numerical sequence as required under Government Accounting and Auditing Manual and Local Treasury Operations Manual.

18.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

c) enforce the COA Order of Execution (COE) by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due to the person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance;

d) ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable prior to the issuance of their office clearances; and

e) require municipal officials and employees to attend training/seminar on the RRPC and RRSA to enhance their understanding and compliance

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) record the proposed correcting entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts;
Cash-Local Treasury 202,239.25
Gov’t Equity 202,239.25
b) identify properly each transaction in preparing Bank Reconciliation Statement as not to include those invalid reconciling items and that he/she be enjoined to have a timely mechanism in recording of transactions and adjustments; and
c) reconcile the cash in bank account pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to establish the accurate balances of all bank accounts. Lastly, bank reconciliation statement should be prepared per bank account number.
observations.managementAction:
Not The same These recommendatio ns were not undertaken by the Treasury Office. Reiterated in Observation No.2, Part II of this Report
The same remedies considered during preparation of the BRS were waiting to be cleared by the Treasury Office. Not Implemented
The new Municipal Accountant Implemented Reiterated in Observation No.2, Part II of this Report
Municipal Accountant assured the Audit Team that these recommendati ons are well-taken and shall be implemented
Fully in CY 2024 Implemented since the CY 2023 BRS were already submitted upon his assumption.
observations.reason: These recommendatio ns were not considered during BRS preparation due to reasons provided heretofore upon his assumption.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Set up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year in compliance with section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in support of the RPT and SET Receivable balances so that reconciliation be made with the MTO; and
b. Reconcile the Abstract of Real Property Tax Collections with the Municipal Treasurer based on Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card to update the list of delinquent taxpayers and to determine the actual balance of the Real Property Tax Receivables.
We also, recommend that the Municipal Assessor is enjoined to furnish the complete and certified List of Taxpayers and taxes due in the beginning of the year as basis for set up.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented The amount set-up in the said Register is still ongoing. While there was still no reconciliation, reconciliation shall start as soon as the registers and list are finalized and formalized.
Not Implemented The Registry is being tried to be established
observations.reason: The preparation of the register is still ongoing; there is yet no precise estimate of the collectibles during the year. The register preparations and list finalization are yet to be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all acquisitions and/or construction of infrastructure projects are recognized as assets of the municipality, in accordance with PPSAS and in line with Section 111 of PD 1445, unless otherwise, indicated in the budget as Subsidy to other LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant is noted trying to trace and make adjustments; management action/reply pending.
observations.reason: Deficiencies in the SL were still being traced using the accounting system; action pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) require the contractor to submit a written justification for the deficiencies noted in the performance of the contract because of failure to comply to the approved plan and specifications; b) impose disciplinary action against officials responsible for the variation orders beyond 10% of the original contract price if warranted; and c) require the Contractor to refund the project deficiency of P377,616.00, otherwise, the Municipal Inspectorate team should be made liable for certifying that the project is 100% completed.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented The Municipal Engineer had tapped the assistance of the Regional Office for reinspection of the projects thru a letter addressed. The letter was received by the then Audit Team Leader, however, the receiving copy, since given and kept by the previous accountant, cannot now be located.
Not Implemented No implementation yet; management action pending.
observations.reason: Receiving copy of letter cannot be located; further action pending and no implementation recorded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Strictly observe IPSAS 1 and Section 125 of the MNGAS for LGUs
in the recording of completed construction projects and the proper
recognition of fund transfers, retention and performance bond;
b) Establish if there were final payments on the above projects and/or
require Certificate of Completion from the Engineering Office and
conduct vouching on the “various” transactions without details.
Thereafter, determine whether they had been completed, reclassify
them accordingly and compute for depreciation;
c) Transfer all funds received from national government agencies to
the Trust Fund books for efficient monitoring;
d) Effect the adjusting journal entries in the books to correct the
balances of the affected accounts; and
e) Maintain subsidiary ledgers/supporting schedules of permanent
accounts in the financial statements to facilitate their review and
analysis and establish accuracy of their balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to properly identify
each transaction in preparing the Bank Reconciliation Statements so as not
to include invalid reconciling items, determine the appropriate liability
accounts where the staled checks shall be charged and thereafter make the
necessary correcting entries in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the differences
between the General Ledger (GL) and Subsidiary Ledger
(SL)/Lapsing Schedule to come up with the correct balances of
PPE accounts;
b) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct inventory-taking of all
PPE items, prepare and submit the required inventory report, and
strictly comply with the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts
pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006;
and
c) Mandate the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to
reconcile the general/subsidiary ledger and the RPCPPE.

We recommended and Management agreed to increase the bond of
the Supply Officer to cover the total property accountability in
accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a) The HRMO ensure that duties and responsibilities assigned to
JOs do not duplicate or overlap with tasks of permanent
employees;
b) Department Heads and the HRMO assess and review the existing
policy guidelines and make the necessary amendments based on
the foregoing observations; and
c) Henceforth, strictly implement the amended guidelines
particularly on the necessity and reasonableness of hiring job
orders and the criteria in the selection of JOs.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a) Observe the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization
and liquidation of cash advances as provided for in Section 89 of
Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;
b) The Municipal Accountant require the authority by the agency
head and the copy of the approved application for bond before
allowing the disbursement; and
c) In case of multiple recipients of cash advances for official
travels, the DV no. should be stated in the Liquidation Reports to
facilitate tracing back to the disbursement voucher to enable
closer monitoring on the promptness of liquidation. This could
be done by appointing, from among the travelling employees,
one who will be accountable for the entire amount of cash
advance.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Property
Insurance Form be accomplished and submitted to the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS, for appropriate action, and a budget be
allocated for the insurance premiums.
We further recommended that the Municipality likewise submits a
photocopy of the Certificate of Registration for each of the vehicles
acquired and as identified.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a) The Municipal Accountant determine the accurate balance of the
DRRM Trust Fund account and make the necessary adjustments
and likewise make appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS.
b) Refund to the DRRMF Account the amount erroneous charges made
under said fund and ensure that charges made pertain to
DRRM activities;
c) Instruct the LDRRM Officer and the Accounting Office to
reconcile monthly their records and reports to ascertain the
propriety of transactions falling under DRRM activities;
d) Require that all transactions are supported by Obligation
Request duly certified by the Municipal Budget Officer; and
e) Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended
balances of QRF and MOOE to the TF books.

We recommended and Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Evaluate the need for unexpended fund balance still maintained
under the Due from NGAs account. Should the funds be proven to
be no longer needed, it should be remitted to the source agencies
as required under existing rules and regulations. If still needed,
Management should request authority from the source agency to
use the money for other related projects of the LGU;
b) Provide individual Subsidiary Ledger of each fund transfer to
determine the balances of each account since every fund transfer
has different source and purpose;
c) Cause the liquidation of all fund transfer for which the purpose
were served and refund the balance, when warranted; and
d) Require the Memorandum of Agreement as additional supporting
documents for the disbursement of fund transfers.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a) The Accounting and Treasury Office continue timely withholding and
remittance to the BIR of the correct amount of taxes to continuously
provide additional income to the government;
b) The Municipal Accountant further analyze the taxes withheld and
remitted to determine the nature and cause of the unremitted balance
and likewise conduct regular review and proper monitoring of the Due to
BIR account to ensure its accuracy; and
c) Strictly adhere with BIR RMC No. 23-2007 and Title X of the NIRC;
otherwise, the responsible officials shall be held liable for the payment of
such penalties, interests and surcharges.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive:
a) enforce the COA Order of Execution (COE) by requiring the
withholding of salaries or other compensation due to the
person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance; and
b) ensure that suspensions and disallowances are settled within
the prescribed period.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain
complete records of schedules for NDs showing the outstanding
balance of each liable person on a quarterly basis.

We recommended that Management:
a) create an Inventory and Disposal Committee pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006. Strict compliance is thus, encouraged considering that this Circular is issued for one-time cleansing and shall not be used to further derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs and/or cleanse subsequent discrepancies or unreconciled balances in the PPE accounts; and
b) require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the lapsing schedule based on the records in the General Ledger (GL) and Subsidiary Ledger (SL) to come up with the correct figures in the lapsing schedule as the basis in the computation of the depreciation expense. And, be reconciled with the records of the property custodian/GSO.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented The Inventory and Disposal Committee was reconstituted.
Not Implemented Reconciliation is still on-going since the new Accountant assumed in February 2024.
observations.reason: The Inventory and Disposal Committee was reconstituted. Reconciliation is still on-going since the new Accountant assumed in February 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) stop the practice of charging to 20% development fund the interest expense from the loan amortization, and
b) require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry:
CIP – Infra Assets 754,809.25
aGov’t Equity 754,809.25
To capitalize interest expense as borrowing costs on loan financed projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management enjoin:
a) the Sanggunian Bayan to re-align/re-appropriate funds/projects in accordance with Section 321 of RA 7160; and
b) the Municipal Engineer faithfully inspect, report, and classify project implementation for proper monitoring.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented The Sanggunian Bayan is now meticulous in passing its Appropriation Ordinances.
Fully Implemented Municipal Engineer had faithfully inspected, reported, and classified project implementation for proper monitoring.
observations.reason: Finds that the same were duly appropriated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the submission of copies of perfected contracts to the Auditor within five (5) days be properly supervised to allow us to timely review the transactions and the commitment of the government for transparency in its transactions.
We also recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit all contracts and its supporting documents of awarded projects as stated under item 2.2 and 2.3 above.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented BAC has exerted efforts to deliver the perfected contracts to the COA within the days prescribed during the year 2023.
Fully Implemented The BAC Secretariat submitted all the found deficient contracts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) to require the Collectors/Treasurer to strictly apply the imposition of interest and penalties on the delinquent tax payers pursuant to Section 168 and 169 of RA 7160; and
b) the Local Finance Committee, to observe reasonable projection of revenues and other collectibles in the ensuing year during preparation of budget proposals.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented The Municipal treasurer has already applied the imposition of interest for the year 2023.
Fully Implemented The LFC has adopted the recommendations made for the year 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a) Management formulate policy guidelines in assessing the necessity of hiring job order employees taking into consideration the reasonable number needed by the different offices and the validity of work to be assigned; 
b) The HRMO require the office where the job order is to be assigned a list of duties and responsibilities before deployment. These may be contained in the Assignment/Deployment Order; and
c) The Municipal Accountant require that all job order contracts and Assignment Order be attached in the disbursement vouchers not only in the first payment of wages. Alternatively, the HRMO may submit hardcopies of the contracts and assignment order together with the master list of job orders indicating all the necessary and relevant information thereon to the Audit Team so that succeeding payments may not be attached in the vouchers in order to save on voluminous resources; and
d) Management comply with the daily minimum wage rate prescribed by the RTWPB-CAR of 2023 as a model in enforcing labor rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented Management prepared a policy guideline as early as 2019 and duly passed Municipal Ordinance and amended in 2023.
Not Implemented The HRMO has required the list of duties and responsibilities from the office/units requesting a service of job order.
Fully Implemented The Municipal Accountant requires that the Job Order contracts are submitted promptly to the Audit Team, while excusing the attachment of the contracts in the vouchers as they were voluminous.
Fully Implemented The Municipality adopted the minimum wage rate.
observations.reason: HRMO required the list of duties from offices; some aspects still being implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) require the MDRRMO to submit a monthly report on sources and utilization of the municipal disaster risk reduction and management fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the audit team; 
b) instruct the LDRRM Officer and the Accounting Office to reconcile monthly their records and reports to ascertain the propriety of transactions falling under DRRM activities; and
c) direct the submission of the Sanggunian’s declaration of state of calamity be if any, and that the Accountant is enjoined to observe the completeness of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented A monthly report has been submitted ever since.
Not Implemented Reconciliation started in 2024.
Fully Implemented The declaration is duly submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Monthly reports prepared and printed starting May 2024; reconciliation only started in 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the concerned LGU Officials and employees who are cooperative officers/member of the Cooperative and association to divest any interests they may have with the latter, otherwise penalties provided in the law may be enforced against erring officials. Moreover, the appointment of BAC members, Secretariat and TWG should be re-evaluated and reorganized with the assurance that no conflict of interests may arise against them.
We further recommended that BAC members strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and GPPB Circular No. 08-2017, to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented The practice of awarding to the cooperative had been stopped as soon as receiving the Audit Observation.
Fully Implemented The BAC observed strictly this recommendation.
observations.reason: No new participation thereafter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) apply the Executive Order No. 77 and other pertinent laws uniformly regardless of rank and position; 
b) require the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement rules and regulations on granting and liquidation of cash advances; and
c) direct the accountable officer to liquidate immediately their unliquidated cash advances and to submit the corresponding liquidation report.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented Management adopted EO 77 in its TEV filings for the year 2023.
Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant will observe the strict granting and liquidation of cash advances. The same findings were still noted during the year 2023.
Not Implemented Accountable Officers will now be prompt in liquidating its cash advances.
observations.reason: Some findings still noted; improvements being implemented in 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management stop charging expenditures contrary to the guidelines laid down under DepED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 so as to prevent possible disallowances in the future.
We also, recommended that the Local School board prepare the budget and Annual Investment Program for Special Education Fund in accordance with the rules and regulations so that fund will be spent for the intended purpose to benefit the students and stakeholders.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented No inconsistent expenditures with the Joint Circular had been charged to the SEF this year 2023.
Not Implemented LSB and AIP will be submitted beginning this year 2024.
observations.reason: LSB AIP were not submitted for year 2023; to be prepared and submitted beginning 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to ensure that the Development Fund is not utilized for expenditures which are expressly disallowed by the Joint Memorandum Circular and to provide all necessary information pertaining to projects budgeted under the fund and their implementation.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented Management implemented the identified observations and the 20% development fund was charged only for allowable expenditures.
observations.reason: The Development Fund was charged appropriately in 2023; no illegal expenditures were charged against 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office and/or the Treasury Office check the appropriateness, validity, and completeness of documents attached to the disbursement vouchers in accordance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree 1445 and related COA rules and regulations before processing or releasing payment.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented The Municipal Accountant and the Treasury Office is now strict in processing the documents and vouchers submitted to them.
observations.reason: Treasury Office is now strict in processing vouchers and documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a) the LCE require the concerned employees/officials to prepare the monthly report of official travels and the monthly report of fuel consumption; and
b) the LCE require the use of Requisition and Issue Voucher (RIV) for all issuances of fuel from the municipal storage tank.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented Management was not aware of the Manual on Fuel Consumption; thus, these reports were not prepared.
Fully Implemented Nonetheless, RIV and other forms had been used ever since.
observations.reason: Management is still looking for ways to prepare the monthly reports to reflect the actual consumption of fuel. RIV usage has been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management demand liquidation of the Petty Cash Fund amounting to ₱37,774.43 from the petty cash fund custodian.
observations.managementAction: Not Fully Implemented Demand was made; the PCF has been settled on June 30, 2024. A balance of P26,000.00 appears at year-end FS.
observations.reason: A balance of P26,000.00 remains at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the recording in the barangay books of accounts and submit to the audit team, the financial transaction documents within 10 working days from receipt thereof, otherwise, inform in writing the SA/ATL of the barangay’s failure to comply with the required submission.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented Municipal Accountant is poor in pushing for a regular barangay meeting with Punong Barangays. Accountant is now pushing for submission; a meeting was held and documents were transmitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some barangay documents were still being noted; transmission to Audit Team later.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly maintain subsidiary and general ledgers of accounts most especially Cash in Bank, Accounts Payable, Due to NGAs, and Trust Liability-DRRM for verification by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The new Municipal Accountant is trying to trace and establish the SL of the accounts using the Municipality’s accounting system. Reiterated in Observation No.1, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Tracing and establishing the correct balances of SLs is ongoing; verification by the audit team pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record immediately in the books of accounts the check disbursements totalling ₱153,517.93 and ensure that all disbursements are properly reported, accounted and corresponding vouchers and supporting documents are submitted on time to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented The Municipal Accountant had been faithful in recording immediately in the books the transactions.
observations.reason: Transactions were recorded immediately; vouchers submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the Chairman of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) Council, ensure that the funds appropriated for mitigation and all trust receipts under the LDRRMF are optimally utilized to ensure preparedness of the municipality in times of disaster.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented Quarterly meeting of MDRRM Council was regularly conducted and among the topic discussed is fund utilization and optimization.
observations.reason: Quarterly meetings were conducted; utilization and optimization noted to be held.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Local School Board, plan its programs and activities and identify priority items where the SEF could be utilized in order to help address existing needs in public schools.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented Personnel from the DepEd present during the last LSB Meeting was consulted and asked to submit their proposal for the fund utilization.
observations.reason: Preparation of proposal by DepEd requested; proposal to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Requisitioning Officer prepare necessary Requisition and Issue Slip for every supplies or goods issued to be summarized by the General Services Officer in the monthly the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued for submission to the Municipal Accountant as basis in recording expenses; and

 management provide a stock room with in the premises for the office/material supplies to properly monitor the status and issuance of stock/inventory.
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record immediately in the books of accounts the check disbursements totalling ₱153,517.93 and ensure that all disbursements are properly reported, accounted and corresponding vouchers and supporting documents are submitted on time to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will submit the disbursement voucher although lacking in some required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012 - 002.
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the Chairman of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) Council, ensure that the funds appropriated for mitigation and all trust receipts under the LDRRMF are optimally utilized to ensure preparedness of the municipality in times of disaster.
observations.managementAction: Management will prepare the LDRRM Investment Plan as a measure to verify programs, projects and activities which are due for implementation.

We recommended that Management: 

a. require the contractor to submit a written justification for the deficiencies noted in the performance of the contract because of failure to comply to the approved plan and specifications.

b. impose disciplinary action                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           against officials responsible for the variation orders beyond 10% of the original contract price if warranted; and

c. require the Contractor to refund the project deficiency of P377,616.00, otherwise, the Municipal Inspectorate team should be made liable for certifying that the project is 100% completed.
observations.managementAction:
No management action.




Management to review the appropriate disciplinary actions imposable.

Management asserted that their justifications for the deficiency was submitted to the COA Regional Office.

observations.recommendation:

effect the proposed adjusting journal entries (Annexes F & H) to correct the balances of accounts in the financial statements;

Transactions were accurately recorded in the books.



Preparation of subsidiary ledgers is ongoing.



Collections for Trust Fund and share of the SEF from real property were immediately deposited in the TF and SEF depository accounts. 

It is impractical to maintain separate booklet for TF because there are very few collections.

The said amount was expended for small items not covered by official receipts; hence, the difficulty in liquidation. Nonetheless, the Custodian will submit liquidation thereof.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

require the contractor to submit a written justification for the deficiencies noted in the performance of the contract because of failure to comply to the approved plan and specifications.

impose disciplinary action against officials responsible for the variation orders beyond 10% of the original contract price if warranted. And

require the Contractor to refund the project deficiency of P377,616.00, otherwise, the Municipal Inspectorate team should be made liable for certifying that the project is 100% completed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
effect the proposed adjusting journal entries (Annexes F & H) to correct the balances of accounts in the financial statements;
ensure accurate recording of transactions in the vouchers and journals including its recapitulation entries, posting of accounts in the General Ledgers and forwarding of balances to the financial statements;
Conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts, effect simultaneous recording of funds affected, trace and locate relevant documents to serve as basis in analyzing the unidentified reconciling items  and prepare necessary adjustments;
determine the appropriate account for the amount of P1,080.43  noted difference under the Trust Fund and the different classification of the amount of P59,535.00 and make the necessary adjustments;
determine project related to the PPE items amounting to P162,233.00 if already completed and effect the necessary transfer to the General Fund. Also, determine the acquisition date and provide the depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation;
support the Quarterly Trial Balance with a schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts;
draw journal entries to record all adjustments directly reflected in the Trial Balance and record the same in the General Journal;
prepare the Trial Balance showing only the net credit or debit balance and  present the accounts  in an ascending order to facilitate financial statements preparation and ensure that the amounts shown in the Trial Balance are the same with those presented in the financial statements; and
record closing entries in the General Journal and prepare the corresponding  Post-closing Trial Balance.

Furthermore, we  recommended that the Municipal Accountant :
determine the amount of sale of bid documents included in the Due to NGAs account including collections for prior years, if any, and reclassify it as Trust Liabilities. Excess collections after payment of honoraria for completed projects, however, should be reverted back to the General Fund as savings pursuant to DBM Circular No. 2004-5A. For succeeding collections, record the same as Trust Liabilities under the General Fund;
ensure accurate and consistent recording of collections and deposits and  follow the pro-forma accounting entries in the recording of share in real property tax and fund transfers from other agencies, as shown on the following table:

For the share of SEF in the real property tax collections, deposits are to be made in the GF and a debit advice to be prepared for the transfer on a monthly basis to avoid confusion.

attach copy of the JEV and appropriate documentary requirements in the General Journal with adequate information to facilitate review and evaluation of the recorded transactions;
record collections and deposits in the Cash Receipts Journal; and
serially number journal entries in the General Journal for ready reference.

the Municipal Accountant ensure the recording in the barangay books of accounts and submit to the audit team, the financial transaction documents within 10 working days from receipt thereof, otherwise, inform in writing the SA/ATL of the barangay’s failure to comply with the required submission.

b. grant cash advance only to permanent employees except for elected officials who may be granted only for traveling expenses; and
c. adhere strictly with the full liquidation of cash advances at the end of the year in accordance with Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated  February 10, 1997.

especially Cash in Bank, Accounts Payable, Due to NGAs, and
Trust Liability-DRRM for verification by the audit team;
b. carefully check accuracy of reported outstanding checks as well as
cancelled checks to ensure reliability of cash balances as shown in
the financial statements; and
c. submit Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRM
Fund in accordance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-
002; and

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. ensure that all acquisitions and/or construction of infrastructure
projects are recognized as assets of the municipality, in accordance
with PPSAS and in line with Section 111 of PD 1445, unless otherwise,
indicated in the budget as Subsidy to other LGUs;
b. effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of
accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record immediately in
the books of accounts the check disbursements totaling ₱153,517.93 and
ensure that all disbursements are properly reported, accounted and
corresponding vouchers and supporting documents are submitted on
time to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the
amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to
Financial Statements pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012 -
002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant forward to the ensuing year the CY 2016 trust fund balances and record subsequent transactions in the books of accounts as well as prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation statements of all bank accounts maintained under trust fund.
observations.managementAction: Trust Fund amount was already taken up in the books and Prior year’s balance of Trust Fund was already forwarded to the ensuing year and BRS were submitted.
observations.reason: However, requested ledgers were not presented/furnished to verify transactions during the year, thus restated in Observation No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the required Notes to the Financial Statements in compliance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended that the LDRRMO and the Municipal accountant strictly comply with the monthly submission of the Report on the sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund in accordance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management implement all programs/projects/activities as embodied in the approved Annual Investment Plan/Supplemental Investment Program in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. require the contractor to submit a written justification for the deficiencies noted in the performance of the contract because of failure to comply to the approved plan and specifications.
b. impose disciplinary action against officials responsible for the variation orders beyond 10% of the original contract price if warranted.
c. require the Contractor to refund the project deficiency of P377,616.00, otherwise, the Municipal Inspectorate team should be made liable for certifying that the project is 100% completed.

observations.reason: Management was not able to collect the corresponding refund from the contractor.
